question: [File: 'File Name: rest/transactions.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getTransactionsByIdOrHash()] Can an unprivileged external user send concurrent requests for the same transaction hash that is not yet in the Redis cache, causing a cache stampede where all concurrent requests simultaneously call getTransactionHash() and pool.queryQuietly(), exhausting DB connections and degrading 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/transactions.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: formatTransactionRows()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a response where every transaction row has a large nft_transfer array, causing formatTransactionRows() to call createNftTransferList() which instantiates NftTransfer and NftTransferViewModel objects for each entry, consuming significant heap memory across 30%+ of Node.js worker processes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/transactions.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: createStakingRewardTransferList()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a /transactions query that returns the maximum limit of transactions all involving staking reward account 800, causing createStakingRewardTransferList() to call getStakingRewardTransferList() with a large timestamps array, issuing a DB query with a large IN-list that degrades 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/transactions.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getTransactionTimestampsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply both account.id and result=fail filters, causing getTransactionTimestampsQuery() to build a NOT IN result condition combined with the four-table FULL OUTER JOIN, producing a query that forces the DB to evaluate a large NOT IN set across multiple joined tables and degrades 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/transactions.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getTransactions()] Can an unprivileged external user send a GET /transactions request with order=asc and no timestamp bounds, causing bindTimestampRange() to anchor the range from the first-ever transaction timestamp forward by maxTransactionsTimestampRangeNs, potentially spanning years of data and forcing a large sequential scan that degrades 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/transactions.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getTransactionsByIdOrHash()] Can an unprivileged external user send a transaction ID lookup with scheduled=true when no scheduled transaction exists, causing mayMissLongTermScheduledTransaction() to return true (line 991) and forcing a second pool.queryQuietly() with an expanded timestamp range, doubling DB load per request and degrading 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/transactions.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getTransactionTimestampsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a debit credit_type filter combined with an account.id filter, causing getTransactionTimestampsQuery() to build a FULL OUTER JOIN between crypto_transfer and token_transfer subqueries each with their own joins to the transaction table, producing a query with four-way join complexity that degrades 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/transactions.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: extractSqlFromTransactionsByIdOrHashRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user send a transaction hash that maps to many rows in the transaction_hash table (hash collision scenario), causing getTransactionHash() to return multiple rows and getTransactionsByTransactionIdsSql() to be called with a large lookupKeys array, producing a complex multi-condition OR query that degrades 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/transactions.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getTransactionsDetails()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger getTransactionsDetails() with a payerAndTimestamps list where all entries share the same payer_account_id but have many distinct consensus_timestamps, causing the CTE query to build a large = any($N) timestamps array that forces a large index scan on the transaction table and degrades 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/transactions.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: doGetTransactions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the pagination link generation in doGetTransactions() by sending requests that always produce a non-empty next link, enabling automated rapid pagination that issues continuous DB queries without rate limiting, degrading 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/transactions.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getTransactionTimestampsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an account.id filter that causes the entityTransactionCondition regex replacement (ctl|t)\\. to produce an incorrect WHERE clause for the entity_transaction subquery, leading to a full table scan on entity_transaction that degrades 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/transactions.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getTransactionsByIdOrHash()] Can an unprivileged external user send many concurrent requests for non-existent transaction IDs, causing getTransactionsByIdOrHash() to execute pool.queryQuietly() for each, receive empty rows, and throw NotFoundError, but the DB queries still consume connection pool slots and degrade 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/transactions.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getTransactions()] Can an unprivileged external user send a GET /transactions request with a timestamp range at the maximum allowed width (maxTimestampRangeNs), causing getTransactionTimestamps() to issue a query spanning the full allowed range against the transaction table, consuming significant DB I/O and degrading 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/transactions.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getTransactionsByTransactionIdsSql()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a transaction ID where the payer account ID is a high-volume account, causing getTransactionsByTransactionIdsSql() to build a query that scans a large portion of the transaction table for that payer, consuming DB CPU and degrading 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/transactions.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getTransactionsByIdOrHash()] Can an unprivileged external user send a transaction ID lookup that returns a transaction with a large inner_transactions array, causing getTransactionsByIdOrHash() to call getTransactionsByTransactionIdsSql() with all inner transaction keys and then pool.queryQuietly() for inner rows, followed by formatTransactionRows() on the combined result, amplifying memory and DB usage across 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/transactions.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: createMaxCustomFeesList()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a response where every transaction has a large max_custom_fees protobuf buffer, causing formatTransactionRows() to call createMaxCustomFeesList() which deserializes CustomFeeLimits protobuf for each transaction, consuming significant CPU across 30%+ of Node.js workers?

[File: 'File Name: rest/transactions.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getTransactionTimestampsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an account.id filter combined with a result=fail filter and a transaction_type filter, causing getTransactionTimestampsQuery() to build a WHERE clause with NOT IN, type = any(), and the synthetic-exclusion condition all combined, producing a query that forces the DB optimizer to choose a suboptimal plan and degrades 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/transactions.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getTransactions()] Can an unprivileged external user send many concurrent GET /transactions requests each with a unique account.id value, causing each request to trigger the four-table FULL OUTER JOIN path in getTransactionTimestampsQuery(), saturating DB CPU with parallel complex join queries and degrading 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/transactions.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getTransferDistinctTimestampsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an account.id filter that causes getTransferDistinctTimestampsQuery() to build a query with a JOIN between the crypto_transfer table and the transaction table using (consensus_timestamp, payer_account_id), and if the account has many transfers, this join produces a large intermediate result that degrades 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/transactions.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getTransactionsByIdOrHash()] Can an unprivileged external user send a transaction hash lookup where the hash resolves to a transaction with scheduled=true, causing getTransactionsByIdOrHashCacheControlHeader() to return an empty header (no caching), ensuring every subsequent request for the same hash hits the DB and degrades 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/transactions.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getTransactionsByIdOrHash()] Can an unprivileged external user send concurrent requests for the same SCHEDULECREATE transaction ID that is within the maxScheduledTransactionConsensusTimestampRangeNs window, causing each request to execute two pool.queryQuietly() calls and return SHORTER_CACHE_CONTROL_HEADER preventing response caching, doubling DB load and degrading 30%+ of nodes?
